roster_note: devflow:code-reviewer excluded on iteration >=2 by its resolved override iterations: "first-only" (config agent_overrides). Recovered by the Step 2.6 shadow, which is never scoped by iterations.

phase3_findings.description: bound=none has no arm. The skill tells later sites to compose <bound-root>/... from the bound= field, but the tool returns the literal token bound=none on an unbound run AND on a foreign-nonce query — both of which this PR keeps legal. Following the prose literally writes and DISPLAYS the draft at a relative none/.devflow/tmp/... under cwd.

The sub-step 2 bind fence is unconditional with no mechanism to detect an existing binding; on the normal path Step 3.6 already bound, so executing it yields binding-already-recorded (rc 1) at the user-presentation step. The documented 'else this presentation write when Step 3.6 was skipped' arm is unreachable (no state file, no nonce).

Sub-step 2 contradicts itself: the read-back enumeration lists 'this presentation write' among sites that must never do a second resolve-main-root.sh resolution, while the fence directly above it is exactly that.

The write-path-empty refusal's binding-INDEPENDENCE is unpinned. Proven by mutation: scoping the empty check under the binding guard keeps every row green while an unbound run with --write-path "" flips from rc 1 to rc 0.

_bound_draft_file re-renders separators via Path, so on native Windows Python the comparand uses backslashes while the skill composes forward slashes — the new string equality could never match on that host.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Path-rendered comparand uses native separators, breaking equality on Windows Python
fix_decisions.evidence: single-source, and not reproducible at this desk (macOS/POSIX) — a speculative cross-platform patch to a comparand shared with the pre-existing #562 readers is higher risk than the recorded deferral

parking_evidence.basis: the claim is plausible but unverifiable in this environment; no Windows host was available to establish it, and unknown is not zero either direction
parking_evidence.failing_input: a bound run on native Windows Python where Path renders backslashes and the caller reports forward slashes
parking_evidence.source: scripts/issue-audit-state.py _bound_draft_file (Path join); reviewer reasoning, no live probe

shadow.prompt_addenda: none — provenance-clean canonical Phase 3 prompts, no prior-findings handoff, no focus/scope clauses

phase3_findings.description: UNANIMOUS (5/5): Step 3.6 is declared the binding site but ships no runnable bind fence — the only fence is in Step 4 sub-step 2, which runs after every audit round dispatches. So at the Step 3.6 file-arm dispatch draft_binding is None, the --write-path cross-check short-circuits, and the guard fails open at its primary site.

bound=none reason=foreign-nonce is conflated with a genuinely unbound run. Foreign-nonce means the run IS bound and only the nonce drifted; the tool still resolves emit-body from the recorded binding, so the skill would overwrite file A while the tool files from file B — silently publishing stale bytes.

§11's 'every later ... site (never re-resolving or recalling it)' is contradicted by two documented exceptions in the same diff: the same-turn presentation write, and the bound=none fallback which deliberately re-resolves.
The cross-check validates a self-reported string while the audited bytes still come from an unvalidated --draft-file, and the diff-added comment, changeset, and §11 all claim otherwise.

Coverage gaps: whitespace-only --write-path (the .strip() arm), right-root/wrong-slug (the other half of _bound_draft_file's join), the inline arm, and a no-state-mutation assertion on the refused dispatch.
A self-referential ordinal count ('a second resolve-main-root.sh call site') in a run.sh comment — the PR #553 ordinal-rot pattern, which the pending Step 3.6 move would have rotted immediately.
